chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH BENCH AT SUKKUR C. P. No. D – 913 of 2025 Date of hearing Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on O/objections at flag ‘A’ 2. For hearing of CMA 3908/2025 (S/A) 3. For hearing of main case 22.07.2025 Mr. Khan Muhammad Sangi, Advocate for petitioner. Syed Naveed Ahmed Shah, D.A.G a/w Inspector Shahzeb, FIA Crime Circle, Sukkur..-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.-.- Pursuant to the Process issued by this Court, Inspector, FIA Crime Circle, Sukkur (respondent No.6) is in attendance and has filed his statement along with a copy of letter dated 21.07.2025, taken on record, and a copy thereof has been supplied to learned counsel for the petitioner. In his statement, respondent No.6 has categorically stated that Enquiry No.66/2024 earlier initiated against the petitioner at FIA Sukkur was closed by the competent authority after thorough deliberations held during the FIA Zonal Board Meeting. However, it is further stated that FIA Sukkur has now received police papers pertaining to FIR No.14 of 2025, registered at P.S. Civil Lines, District Larkana, along with Court orders directing transfer of investigation from the said police station to FIA Sukkur. Consequently, a fresh Enquiry bearing No.32/2025 has been initiated by the competent authority, which is presently under process and no conclusive findings have been drawn as yet. From the record and submissions made, it appears that the current proceedings are only at the preliminary enquiry stage and no formal accusation or adverse action has yet been taken against the petitioner. Therefore, no case for interference by this Court at this juncture is made out.

From the record and submissions made, it appears that the current proceedings are only at the preliminary enquiry stage and no formal accusation or adverse action has yet been taken against the petitioner. Therefore, no case for interference by this Court at this juncture is made out. Accordingly, the instant petition stands disposed of along with the listed application. However, in view of the statement made by respondent No.6, it is directed that the petitioner shall not be arrested in connection with Enquiry bearing No.32/2025, till its culmination, without prior permission of the competent authority and due process of law. J U D G E J U D G E AHMAD/PS
